discovery.run_summary: Risk-off tone driven by software/AI spending concerns after ServiceNow and IBM results, with ORCL -6% and MSFT -4% dragging XLK down 1.4%. Defensive sectors (XLU, XLP, XLI) and energy (oil over $105 on Hormuz tensions) outperforming. Several mid-cap idiosyncratic blowups (MEDP, WEX) appear earnings-driven rather than macro.

notable_index_moves: XLK -1.43% with software leading down on ServiceNow/IBM results
XLU +2.74% and XLP +1.67% — classic defensive rotation
XLE +0.75% modest given Brent >$105 — suggests market skeptical oil spike is durable or already priced in
VIX +2.3% to 19.36, elevated but not panicked
SMH +1.05% bucking tech weakness on TXN +18% AI demand beat — bifurcation between semis and software

discoveries.name: Medpace Holdings
discoveries.mechanism: Likely earnings/guidance reaction — magnitude suggests guidance cut or bookings miss in CRO space. Could drag peers (ICLR, IQV, CRL) via sympathy.
discoveries.catalyst: Presumed Q1 earnings (not confirmed in news feed provided)
research_pointers: Confirm MEDP Q1 print — book-to-bill, backlog, revenue guidance
Check if cancellations from biotech customers (XBI -2% today) are cited
Look at ICLR, IQV, CRL, SYNH reactions for sympathy-selling opportunity or confirmation of sector-wide issue
Historical pattern: MEDP has had sharp one-day drops before that recovered within a quarter
discoveries.what_would_falsify: If management cited a structural demand shift in biopharma R&D (not just timing/lumpiness), the drop would be justified and CRO peers should be sold, not bought.

discoveries.name: WEX Inc.
discoveries.mechanism: Fleet card/payments company — likely earnings miss or guide-down. Fuel price volatility from Hormuz tensions may have pressured hedge book or spending trends.
research_pointers: Verify Q1 earnings details — fuel price sensitivity, travel segment (corporate travel), health segment
Check if guidance cut is fuel-price-volatility driven (possibly transient) vs. volume/customer-loss driven (structural)
Compare to FLEETCOR/Corpay (CPAY) for read-through
5-day -11.8% suggests pre-earnings weakness — insider activity worth checking
discoveries.what_would_falsify: Customer losses or competitive pricing pressure cited as reasons — that would be structural and justify the move.

discoveries.name: Repligen
discoveries.mechanism: Bioprocessing supplier caught in XBI -2% weakness plus possible MEDP sympathy (both tied to biopharma R&D spend). Also possible Regeneron/Trump drug pricing deal fears spreading to bioprocessing suppliers.
discoveries.catalyst: Sector weakness + Regeneron drug pricing deal news raising biopharma margin concerns
research_pointers: Check if RGEN has earnings imminent
Assess exposure to GLP-1 manufacturing capacity buildout (Lilly, Novo) — drug pricing pressure could slow capex
Compare to DHR, TMO, SRPT bioprocessing segments
Is this a sympathy move tied to MEDP CRO read-through? If so, arguably overdone since CRO demand ≠ bioprocessing demand
discoveries.what_would_falsify: If a specific negative read on RGEN's own customer orders emerges, the move is justified.

discoveries.name: Kratos Defense
discoveries.mechanism: Defense weakness odd given active Iran/Hormuz conflict — ITA flat. 5-day -12% suggests specific negative news or profit-taking after run. Normal volume argues against fresh catalyst.
research_pointers: Check for contract loss, program cancellation, or short report
DCO +4.4% same sector — divergence suggests KTOS-specific issue
Valuation had gotten rich; could simply be multiple compression on risk-off day
Earnings date approaching?
discoveries.what_would_falsify: Specific bad news (contract loss, guidance concern) would justify; absence would suggest overdone given supportive geopolitical backdrop.

sympathy + buyout news compounds narrative of cost pressure

catalyst_chains.primary_event: Regeneron Trump pricing deal — pattern of pharma concessions continues

discovery.no_signals_note: Truth Social feed empty this run. Main Trump-adjacent market factor is the Hormuz 'shoot and kill' order (already in oil prices) and the ongoing drug pricing deal cadence (REGN today).

ai_announcements.headline: OpenAI releases GPT-5.5, moving toward AI 'super app'
ai_announcements.summary: New frontier model with broader capabilities; positioning ChatGPT as a super app consolidating many workflows.

affected_stocks.counter_thesis: WEX's core fleet/corporate payments business has deep integration with fuel networks and B2B payment rails; GPT-5.5 doesn't replace payment processing. Decline more likely driven by earnings/guidance (pre-announcement is 4/24) or macro fleet concerns, not AI.
research_pointers: Check if WEX pre-announced Q1 or cut guidance today
Fuel price sensitivity / fleet customer metrics
Any mention of AI disruption on recent calls - likely none

affected_stocks.counter_thesis: For-profit education faces a narrative threat from LLMs (self-learning), but PRDO serves credential-seeking adult learners where accreditation and federal aid eligibility are the moat, not content delivery. GPT-5.5 headline could be sympathy selling.
research_pointers: Enrollment trends at CTU/AIU
Title IV funding exposure
Whether selloff correlates with broader education sector

ai_announcements.headline: Google Workspace Intelligence adds AI 'office intern' automations
ai_announcements.summary: Google embedding AI agents across Workspace for automated office tasks.

ai_analysis.no_signals_note: Largest movers today (MEDP -22.66%, WEX -16.31%, RGEN -8.31%) are overwhelmingly likely earnings-driven, not AI-driven. MEDP is a CRO reporting around this date; RGEN is bioprocessing equipment - neither has meaningful exposure to GPT-5.5 or Workspace AI. Defense names (KTOS, MRCY) moving on sector/budget news unrelated to today's AI announcements. No Anthropic announcements in feed. Recommend Michael verify earnings calendar before attributing any of these moves to AI narrative.
